title: Étude sur l'iconographie du Bayon (Frontons et linteaux) - Persée
highlights: DÂGENS Cette étude qui fait partie d'une enquête systématique sur l'iconographie des monuments khmers a été conçue comme un complément à l'histoire architecturale du Bayon telle que l'a présentée M. Dumarçay dans un travail dont nous considérons les conclusions comme admises (1). Elle comporte un répertoire des frontons et des linteaux, un commentaire explicitant certaines interprétations et regroupant les grands thèmes et, enfin, une troisième partie consacrée à l'étude des rapports existant entre le monument et son iconographie. On n'y cherchera point de conclusions générales sur l'iconographie des monuments de Jayavarman VII, conclusions que nous jugerions prématurées dans l'état actuel de la recherche. Pour le Bayon pro

title: Towers on the Earthen Foundation: New Insights by the Excavation and Boring Survey at the Bayon Temple
highlights: Bayon temple, built in the latter half of the 12th century, is one representative temple of the Angkor monuments. To shed light on the foundation structure of the central towers  ...    carried out surveys for evaluating the structural stability of the central terrace and the central group of towers.  A part of the upper masonry of the central tower, made of a dry masonry of sandstone blocks, collapsed in 1932 and was subject to strengthening work by l’École Française de d’Extrême-Orient (EFEO) in 1933. The strengthening materials that were used at the time have deteriorated, however, such that it has become necessary to consider conducting a re-restoration. title: The Amazing Mechanism of the Standing for 700years of the Tall Masonry Stone Tower of 32m in height on Manmade Sandy High Mound by Shallow Direct Foundation, Bayon Temple, Angkor, Cambodia
highlights:
Soils and foundation of Bayon temple of Angkor Thom has been studied since 1994 by Japanese Government Team for Safeguarding Angkor (JSA). The main tower of Bayon of 32m in height from the base foundation mound consists of manmade fill of 14 m in thickness.
